Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Separate Mevo OCR display from GSPro relay for direct LM connections

The Flightscope Mevo Gen 2 connects to GSPro directly via the E6
protocol — it doesn't need Birdman to relay shot data. The OCR
was conflicting with the native connection.

New setting: mevo.send_to_gspro (default: false)
- When disabled (default): Mevo OCR captures data for display in
  Birdman's UI and OBS overlay only. GSPro receives shots directly
  from the Flightscope app.
- When enabled: Birdman relays Mevo data to GSPro via OpenAPI
  (for launch monitors without native GSPro support).

UI shows "Send Mevo data to GSPro" checkbox with hint text
"Disable if LM connects to GSPro directly".

commit.message:
fix(firmware): CE-2, R12, M1-M3, M2 CCCD discovery, on_disc_chr spinlock

CE-2: default_role_for_new_ring() scans occupied roles instead of using
  insertion index — forget+re-pair now assigns CURSOR if it is unoccupied
  rather than repeating the last-used index's role

R12: hal_storage_set / hal_storage_commit return values now checked and
  logged in flush_to_nvs — NVS write failures are visible in diagnostics

M2: replace val_handle+1 CCCD assumption with proper GATT descriptor
  discovery — add on_disc_dsc callback that searches [val_handle,
  val_handle+HID_DSC_SEARCH_RANGE] for UUID 0x2902 and subscribes after
  confirmation; CCCD write and subscribed flag moved into descriptor
  discovery completion handler

on_disc_chr: protect hid_report_handle write under RINGS_LOCK (future-proof
  against cross-core readers of those fields)

M1: BLE_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 named constant replaces magic 30000
M3: BLE_SCAN_INTERVAL=0x50 / BLE_SCAN_WINDOW=0x30 named constants with
  rationale; HID_DSC_SEARCH_RANGE=8 named constant for descriptor search range

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Align GSPro message format exactly with MLM2PRO-GSPro-Connector

Verified against springbok/MLM2PRO-GSPro-Connector ball_data.py
to_gspro() — the defacto standard for GSPro Open Connect v1.

Key corrections from our previous R10-based format:
- Restore Backspin, SideSpin, CarryDistance in BallData
- Restore LaunchMonitorIsReady, LaunchMonitorBallDetected, IsHeartBeat
  in ShotDataOptions (MLM2PRO connector includes these in shots)
- Always include full ClubData section with all fields (Speed,
  AngleOfAttack, FaceToTarget, Lie, Loft, Path, SpeedAtImpact,
  VerticalFaceImpact, HorizontalFaceImpact, ClosureRate)
- ContainsClubData always True (matching MLM2PRO behavior)
- Note: MLM2PRO uses "Backspin" (not "BackSpin") — matched exactly

Shot send remains fire-and-forget (non-blocking recv).

commit.message:
Parallel compression, progress reporting, and verification

- Parallel segment-based indexing: split large files across nproc threads
  for both A and B, with query-time offset filtering for the strictly-before
  invariant (enables parallel B chunking + SHA-256)
- Zero-copy LIT encoding: write directly from mmap to patch file, eliminating
  intermediate heap buffer copies
- Progress callback API (quine_progress_fn) with per-step wall time in CLI
- Post-compression verification: decompress to tmpdir and byte-compare
- Fix VmPeak→VmHWM for accurate RSS measurement
- Fix uint32_t overflow in segment offsets for files >4 GB
- Streaming file comparison (constant memory) for verification
- All build artifacts now output to build/ directory
- 5 new tests for offset filtering, parallel work balance, and dedup
- Updated README with new project layout, build targets, and callback API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Align GSPro message format with R10/MLM2PRO connector for compatibility

Key differences found by comparing with the upstream R10 connector
(alleexx/gspro-garmin-connect-v2 gsProConnect.js):

Shot messages:
- Remove BackSpin/SideSpin from BallData (connector only sends Speed,
  SpinAxis, TotalSpin, HLA, VLA)
- Remove LaunchMonitorIsReady/LaunchMonitorBallDetected/IsHeartBeat
  from ShotDataOptions (only ContainsBallData/ContainsClubData)
- These extra fields may have been confusing GSPro's shot processing

Send behavior:
- Switch to fire-and-forget (non-blocking recv) matching connector's
  socket.write() with no response wait
- Non-blocking drain of any pending response for logging only
- Eliminates the 10s blocking recv that was killing connections

Heartbeat format unchanged (still has IsHeartBeat/LaunchMonitorIsReady).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reimage/reboot all not working — node_action too slow for 392 nodes

The old node_action processed nodes ONE BY ONE through the store:
1. Look up ManagedNode by internal ID in store
2. Look up account by accountId
3. Run through scheduler (concurrency=1 per account)
4. Call performNodeAction for 1 node
5. Repeat 392 times — extremely slow, many nodes skipped (stale store)

New bulk_node_action in orchestrator:
1. Groups nodes by account + pool from the store
2. For each pool: calls listNodes via Batch API (fresh data, not stale store)
3. Calls performNodeAction for each node directly (no scheduler queue)
4. Reports: "Reimaged 380/392 nodes (8 skipped, 4 failed)"

Nodes page now dispatches bulk_node_action instead of node_action for all
actions (reboot, reimage, disableScheduling, enableScheduling).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

code that imports them, so native builds skip these files.

commit.message:
Fix GSPro connection drops by reducing recv timeout and handling gracefully

The 10s socket.recv() timeout was killing the connection when GSPro
didn't respond promptly. After timeout, the connection was marked failed,
GSPro closed its end, and reconnection attempts were refused.

Changes:
- Split send and recv into separate try blocks
- Reduce recv timeout to 2s (from 10s inherited socket timeout)
- Treat recv TimeoutError as likely success (GSPro often processes
  shots without responding) — connection stays alive
- Only kill connection on actual send failures or recv OSError
- Restore socket timeout after recv attempt

This prevents the cascade: timeout → disconnect → refused → backoff
that was causing shots to stop working after 2-3 hits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix mul_bounds producing NaN from 0 * inf (#3267)

* Fix mul_bounds producing NaN from 0 * inf

In IEEE 754, 0 * inf = NaN, but in interval arithmetic if an endpoint
is zero then the product is zero. This caused get_bounds() to return
NaN for expressions like nonneg_var * nonpos_var (bounds [0,inf] * [-inf,0]).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Suppress RuntimeWarning from 0 * inf in mul_bounds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Parallelize Mevo OCR with ThreadPoolExecutor for ~5x speedup

Tesseract OCR on 18 ROIs sequentially took ~6 seconds. Now uses a
thread pool (6 workers) to OCR multiple ROIs concurrently. Tesseract
releases the GIL during its C processing so threads give real
parallelism. Expected improvement: ~6s → ~1-2s.

Refactored read_metrics to dispatch _read_single_roi per ROI. Each
includes its own sanity check and logging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
